summary.intent: Fix MiniMax provider bugs (system prompt delivery, tool calling) reported in GitHub issues #578, #582, #592, and add live API integration tests to prevent regressions, with a model discovery mechanism to detect new models.
summary.outcome: Fixed the MiniMax system prompt delivery bug by prepending system content to the first user message instead of using a silently-ignored top-level `system` field; created 12 live API integration tests covering all three issues plus model discovery; all 270 unit tests and 12 integration tests pass.

repo: MiniMax rejects `role: 'system'` in the messages array (error 2013) AND silently ignores a non-standard top-level `system` JSON field — system prompts must be prepended to the first user message with markers like `[System Instructions]`.
The workspace clippy config denies `.expect()` and `.unwrap()` globally; integration test files need `#![allow(clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::expect_used)]` at the top.
Integration tests use `#[ignore]` so they don't run in CI by default; run with `cargo test --test minimax_integration -- --ignored` and a live API key.

The internal tool format for `LlmProvider` is flat (`{name, description, parameters}`) not nested OpenAI format (`{type: 'function', function: {name, ...}}`); using the wrong format causes models to return tool calls as plain text.
Provider integration tests live in `crates/providers/tests/` as separate test binaries, not inline `#[cfg(test)]` modules.
The nightly formatter (`cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 fmt`) must be used; stable fmt produces different output that fails nightly clippy checks.

code.finding: Renamed `apply_top_level_system_prompt` to `apply_system_prompt_rewrite` and changed behavior: instead of top-level `system` field, system content is prepended to the first user message. The method `requires_top_level_system_prompt` was renamed to `rejects_system_role`. All 3 call sites (probe, complete, stream_with_tools_sse) updated.

New file with 12 live API integration tests. Requires `MINIMAX_API_KEY` env var. Covers system prompt delivery (streaming + non-streaming), tool call round trips, optional array params, multi-turn tool use, model catalog health checks, and new model detection.

workflow: Run integration tests against real APIs to catch bugs that mocks miss — all three GitHub issues were 'closed' but the bugs were still present because only mocks were used.
Model catalog drift detection tests (checking known models are still live + detecting unexpected new ones) are valuable for provider maintenance.
When the workspace has strict lint rules, add `#![allow(...)]` at the file level for test files rather than per-function.
Collapsible `if` / `if let` chains trigger clippy warnings; prefer `if let Some(x) = foo && condition { }` style.

friction: The previous 'fix' for MiniMax system prompts (top-level `system` field) was silently wrong — MiniMax accepted the request without error but ignored the field, making it hard to detect without live API tests.
Tool format mismatch (flat internal vs nested OpenAI) caused tool calls to come back as plain text with no error — required reading provider internals to diagnose.
Clippy denied `.unwrap()`/`.expect()` workspace-wide, requiring `#![allow]` attributes added after initial test file was written.
CUDA build failure on macOS blocked running clippy on the full workspace; had to scope clippy to the providers crate specifically.
The nightly formatter reformatted code in a way that then conflicted with clippy expectations, requiring an extra format+lint iteration.
open_items: Same provider integration test pattern needs to be applied to all other providers (user explicitly said 'we'll do the same things with other providers, I'll add all API keys').
CI secret `MINIMAX_API_KEY` needs to be added to the repo for integration tests to run in CI jobs.
Model discovery mechanism exists in tests but no automated alerting when new MiniMax models appear — needs a CI job or scheduled check.
The `#582` optional array params fix should be verified more thoroughly — the test passes but the underlying serialization path for null arrays was not explicitly shown as fixed in the transcript.
